g.text: advance() — the ONLY place line/column update (scanner.py)
Common Pitfall: forgetting column reset on newline
Token position = position of FIRST character (snapshotted at _begin_token)
self.column += 1 # ← any other char: bump column only

text.tspan: # ← any other char: bump column only

div.p: \t hits the else branch — treated identically to any non-newline character: column += 1. '\t' does NOT advance by tab-stop width (4 or 8). Column advances by exactly 1. Input: x\tb

ul.li: step 3: consume b → col 4 Token b is reported at column 3, not column 5.

div.p: WRONG — bypassing advance(): python self.current += 1 # skips line/column tracking!

After \n on line 1 col 5, column stays at 6 instead of resetting to 1. Every token on line 2 gets a wrong column. Drift compounds — line 50 tokens show col ~250 instead of ~1. CORRECT — always call advance() to consume characters. Position tracking is centralised: one function, one truth.
